title: [SV] Make `VerbatimExpr` accept arbitrary result types
body:
Is there a technical reason why the `VerbatimExpr` of the SV dialect has a result type restricted to `AnySignlessInteger`? Would it make sense to generalize this to accept `AnyType`?

As a concrete example, I would like to have a piece of verbatim SV act as an expression that returns a FIRRTL type. It feels like the `VerbatimExpr` op would be the perfect thing to use:
```
%0 = sv.verbatim.expr "foo.bar" : () -> !firrtl.uint<42>  // could be a bundle, vector, any other type
%1 = firrtl.add %0, %otherStuff : (!firrtl.uint<42>, !firrtl.uint<42>) -> !firrtl.uint<43>
```
